questions.question: What happens when blood sugar crashes during the night after eating sugary foods before bed?

options.text: You sleep more deeply
Your body releases cortisol, which can wake you up between 2-4 AM
Melatonin production increases
REM sleep is extended

questions.explanation: When blood sugar crashes after a spike, your body releases cortisol (a stress hormone) to bring it back up. This cortisol surge can wake you up -- often between 2 AM and 4 AM -- and make it hard to fall back asleep.

questions.question: What role does magnesium play in supporting sleep?

options.text: It increases alertness during the day
It regulates the neurotransmitter GABA, relaxes muscles, and supports melatonin production
It speeds up caffeine metabolism
It raises body temperature for better sleep

questions.explanation: Magnesium helps regulate GABA (which calms nervous system activity), relaxes muscles, and supports your body's natural melatonin production. Food sources include leafy greens, almonds, and dark chocolate.

questions.question: What is the average half-life of caffeine?

options.text: 2-3 hours
4-5 hours
7-9 hours
12-14 hours

questions.explanation: Caffeine has an average half-life of 7-9 hours (some studies show 5-9 hours depending on individual metabolism). A coffee at 2 PM can still have half its caffeine active at 10 PM.

questions.question: How does alcohol affect sleep quality?

options.text: It improves both falling asleep and sleep quality
It helps you fall asleep faster but significantly reduces REM sleep quality
It has no effect on sleep stages
It only affects deep sleep, not REM sleep

questions.explanation: Alcohol is a sedative that can help you fall asleep faster, but as your body metabolizes it during the night, it significantly reduces REM sleep -- the stage critical for emotional processing and memory consolidation.

questions.question: What is tryptophan and why is it important for sleep?

options.text: A vitamin that increases energy
An amino acid that converts to serotonin and then melatonin
A mineral that relaxes muscles
A hormone released during deep sleep

questions.explanation: Tryptophan is an amino acid that your body converts into serotonin and then melatonin -- the hormone that signals your brain it is time to sleep. Food sources include turkey, oats, nuts, eggs, and tofu.
